title: Kotiopettajattaren romaani (Jane Eyre)
summaries: "Kotiopettajattaren romaani (Jane Eyre)" by Charlotte Brontë is a novel published in 1847. It follows Jane Eyre from her abusive childhood through her education and eventual position as governess at Thornfield Hall, where she falls in love with the mysterious Mr. Rochester. Told through intimate first-person narrative, the novel traces Jane's moral and spiritual development while addressing themes of class, religion, and women's independence. This groundbreaking work revolutionized fiction by exploring the private consciousness of its protagonist with unprecedented psychological intensity. (This is an automatically generated summary.)
